wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Ubar ride-sharing platform monorepo. Explain the project overview, architecture, and core concepts. Document installation requirements including Node.js version, database setup with Supabase, and environment configuration. Provide step-by-step instructions for cloning the repository, installing dependencies, setting up development environment, and running all three applications (passenger, driver, admin). Include initial configuration steps for Supabase authentication, database migrations with Prisma, and basic testing procedures. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monorepo Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the monorepo structure section focused on Ubar's workspace architecture. Explain the purpose of organizing three separate Next.js applications (passenger, driver, admin) within a single repository using npm workspaces. Document the shared packages strategy in the packages directory including shared-types, shared-ui, shared-utils, prisma, and real-time services. Detail how dependency management works across applications, TypeScript configuration sharing, and build orchestration. Include practical examples of how to add new packages, manage cross-package dependencies, and maintain consistency across applications. Address the benefits of this approach for code reuse, type safety, and development workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Passenger Application authentication system. Document the login and registration workflows, Supabase integration patterns, session management, and role-based access control implementation. Explain the authentication flow from user interface to database validation, including form handling, error states, and security measures. Cover the provider setup for global authentication state, protected route implementation, and user profile management. Include code examples of authentication hooks, API route handlers, and UI component integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Dashboard & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Driver Dashboard and management system. Document the main dashboard interface, driver status management (online/offline), real-time trip notifications, performance metrics display, and quick action buttons. Explain the dashboard layout, responsive design patterns, loading states handling, and real-time data updates. Include implementation details for status broadcasting, notification systems, and dashboard state management. Address mobile-first design considerations, battery optimization for background operation, and user experience best practices for drivers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Admin Dashboard, the central hub for platform monitoring and overview. Document the dashboard layout structure, key metrics display, real-time system status indicators, and navigation components. Explain how administrators can monitor platform health, view active users, track trip statistics, and access quick management tools. Include details on data visualization components, performance metrics, and alert systems. Address responsive design considerations and accessibility features for administrative interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Types & Interfaces
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Shared Types package that defines common TypeScript interfaces and type definitions used across all Ubar applications. Document user role types, trip status enums, payment models, driver/passenger interfaces, and shared data structures. Explain type inheritance patterns, validation schemas, and how these types ensure consistency between frontend and backend. Include examples of type usage in API responses, database operations, and component props. Address type versioning strategies and backward compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supabase Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Supabase integration across all applications. Document the client configuration, environment setup, and connection management patterns. Explain the differences between client-side and server-side Supabase implementations, including when to use each approach. Cover authentication provider setup, database connections, real-time subscriptions, and storage configurations. Include examples of proper initialization, error handling, and connection pooling strategies. Address security considerations for API keys, environment variables, and client/server separation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the WebSocket implementation in the Ubar real-time system. Document connection establishment, authentication integration with Supabase, connection lifecycle management, and error handling strategies. Explain the provider architecture used across all applications, connection pooling mechanisms, and client-side WebSocket management. Include configuration options, connection parameters, and security considerations. Provide examples of establishing connections, handling connection states, and implementing reconnection logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the payment processing system handling trip payments, payment method management, and transaction lifecycle. Document the payment initiation flow from passenger booking confirmation through payment gateway integration. Explain payment validation, authorization processes, and error handling mechanisms. Include coverage of payment status tracking, webhook processing, and payment confirmation workflows. Address payment security measures, PCI compliance requirements, and sensitive data handling. Document payment retry logic, timeout handling, and failure recovery strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for authentication endpoints across all applications. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication flow for login and register endpoints. Include detailed specifications for user registration with role-based setup, login authentication with JWT tokens, session management, and password handling. Provide concrete examples of request payloads including user credentials, response formats with token structures, and error responses for authentication failures. Document security considerations, rate limiting, and best practices for secure authentication implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Driver Management system. Document the complete driver lifecycle from registration to approval, including document verification workflows, background check processes, and approval/rejection mechanisms. Detail driver account status management including blocking, unblocking, and suspension features. Explain driver profile editing capabilities, vehicle information management, and performance metrics tracking. Include search and filtering functionality for drivers, bulk operations, and audit logging for all driver-related administrative actions. Address security considerations, data privacy compliance, and best practices for driver administration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Request Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the trip request creation workflow. Document the complete process of submitting a new trip request, including passenger input validation, location services integration, driver matching algorithms, and pricing calculations. Explain the API endpoint structure, request/response schemas, error handling patterns, and business logic for trip validation. Include examples of trip request forms, location selection interfaces, and real-time availability checks. Cover data models for trip requests, state management patterns, and integration with the driver dispatch system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Acceptance Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Trip Acceptance Workflow. Document how drivers receive trip requests, view passenger details and pickup locations, and accept or reject trips. Explain the acceptance API endpoint, request validation, driver availability checks, and real-time notification system. Include code examples showing trip request handling, passenger information display, and location-based matching logic. Address edge cases like multiple simultaneous requests, driver capacity limits, and network timeout scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core UI components library including buttons, form inputs, cards, modals, and basic layout elements. Document component APIs with prop interfaces, default values, and event handlers. Include usage examples with code snippets demonstrating proper implementation patterns across all three applications (passenger, driver, admin). Address styling customization options, theme integration, accessibility features, and responsive design considerations. Provide guidelines for component composition and reusability patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management & Lifecycle
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the connection management system that handles WebSocket connections and real-time communication lifecycle. Document connection establishment, authentication integration with Supabase, connection pooling strategies, automatic reconnection mechanisms, and connection state management. Include error handling patterns, timeout configurations, and monitoring connection health. Provide examples of connection initialization, cleanup procedures, and debugging techniques for connection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role Definitions & User Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for role definitions and user type implementations in the Ubar system. Document the base User entity structure, role-specific extensions (Passenger, Driver, Admin), and their unique properties and validation rules. Explain how roles are defined in the shared types package, database schema relationships, and TypeScript interfaces. Include examples of role-specific registration flows, required fields per role, and data validation patterns. Address role inheritance patterns and how new roles can be added to the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Creation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for trip creation endpoints. Document the passenger trip booking workflow including request schema validation, driver matching algorithms, pricing calculation, and trip initialization. Include comprehensive examples of trip creation requests with pickup/dropoff locations, passenger preferences, and payment method selection. Document response formats for successful bookings, driver assignment confirmations, and error responses for unavailable drivers or invalid locations. Address rate limiting, authentication requirements, and data validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Administration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for driver administration endpoints covering driver lifecycle management, approval workflows, and account control. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for driver CRUD operations, approval/rejection processes, blocking/unblocking drivers, and status modifications. Include detailed specifications for driver onboarding workflows, document verification processes, approval decision APIs, and driver performance tracking. Provide concrete examples of driver registration validation, approval workflow requests, bulk driver operations, and driver status change operations. Document admin-only permissions and audit logging requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Approval Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the driver approval workflow system. Document the complete approval process from driver registration to account activation, including document verification steps, background check integration, and manual review processes. Explain the approve and reject API endpoints, their request/response schemas, and error handling mechanisms. Detail the dashboard interface for reviewing driver applications, viewing submitted documents, and making approval decisions. Include workflow state transitions, notification systems for drivers, and audit trails for approval decisions. Address security considerations for sensitive document handling and compliance requirements for driver vetting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Ubar monorepo system. Describe the high-level design principles, monorepo structure with three separate Next.js applications sharing common infrastructure. Document the technology stack including Next.js 14+, React Server Components, Supabase for authentication and database, Prisma ORM, and real-time communication patterns. Explain the separation of concerns between passenger, driver, and admin applications, shared packages strategy, and data flow architecture. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions, deployment topology, and integration points with external services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack section focused on Ubar's core technologies and frameworks. Document the primary stack including Next.js 14+ with App Router and Server Components, React 18+, TypeScript for type safety, Supabase for authentication and database services, Prisma ORM for database access, and real-time communication patterns. Explain the rationale behind each technology choice, version compatibility requirements, and integration patterns. Include installation prerequisites, environment setup requirements, and how these technologies work together to form the complete system architecture. Address performance considerations, scalability implications, and alternative technology options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Booking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the trip booking system within the passenger application. Document the complete trip lifecycle from request creation to completion, including trip request submission, status tracking, cancellation handling, and history management. Explain the business logic for trip validation, driver matching algorithms, pricing calculations, and real-time status updates. Cover the API endpoints for trip operations, data models, state management patterns, and error handling strategies. Include examples of trip request forms, status monitoring components, and history display interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Trip Lifecycle Management system. Document the complete trip workflow from acceptance to completion, including trip request handling, navigation integration, passenger pickup procedures, route tracking, and trip completion processes. Explain the trip state machine, API endpoints for trip actions, real-time status synchronization, and error handling scenarios. Include detailed coverage of trip validation, passenger information display, fare calculation, and trip history recording. Address edge cases like trip cancellation, emergency situations, and connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the User Management System, covering both driver and passenger administration capabilities. Document user account lifecycle management, profile editing, role assignment, and account status control. Explain driver approval workflows including document verification, background checks, and approval/rejection processes. Detail passenger account management features including suspension, deletion, and activity monitoring. Include search and filtering capabilities, bulk operations, and audit logging for all user-related actions. Address security considerations, data privacy compliance, and administrative best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Shared UI Components library that provides reusable interface elements across all Ubar applications. Document button components, form inputs, navigation elements, data tables, modal dialogs, and specialized components like trip cards and driver profiles. Include component APIs, prop interfaces, styling customization options, and accessibility features. Provide usage examples with code snippets demonstrating proper implementation patterns. Address responsive design considerations, theme integration, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Based Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the role-based access control system implementing passenger, driver, and admin roles. Document the user registration flows specific to each role, including validation rules, required fields, and approval workflows. Explain how roles are assigned during registration, how permissions are checked on protected routes, and how middleware enforces access controls. Include examples of role-specific API endpoints, route protection patterns, and permission checking utilities. Address role switching scenarios, temporary permissions, and audit logging for access attempts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Location Tracking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the real-time location tracking system. Document driver location broadcasting, passenger location updates, nearby driver discovery algorithms, and geospatial queries. Explain location data structure, update frequency optimization, and battery usage considerations. Cover the location API endpoints, data validation, and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of implementing location broadcasting, subscribing to location updates, and calculating distances between drivers and passengers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Earnings Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the driver earnings calculation and management system. Document earnings calculation algorithms including base fare, distance pricing, time-based charges, surge pricing, and bonus structures. Explain earnings distribution between platform fees and driver payouts. Cover earnings history tracking, performance metrics, and payout scheduling. Include documentation for earnings reports, tax calculations, and withdrawal processing. Address earnings disputes, adjustments, and audit trails for financial transparency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for trip management endpoints across passenger, driver, and admin applications. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for trip creation, acceptance, completion, cancellation, and history retrieval. Include detailed specifications for trip lifecycle management, status transitions, location tracking integration, and real-time updates. Provide concrete examples of trip booking requests, driver acceptance workflows, trip completion processes, and historical data queries. Document error handling for trip conflicts, availability issues, and state validation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Passenger Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Passenger Management system. Cover passenger account oversight including profile viewing, account suspension, and deletion capabilities. Document passenger activity monitoring features, trip history access, and behavior analysis tools. Explain search and filtering functionality for passengers, bulk account operations, and audit logging for administrative actions. Include passenger rating system oversight, complaint handling workflows, and account recovery processes. Address data privacy considerations, user consent management, and compliance requirements for passenger data handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for trip status management and tracking. Document the real-time status updates, state transitions, and lifecycle management of trips from booking to completion. Explain the status flow (pending, accepted, in-progress, completed, cancelled), real-time communication patterns, and UI components for status display. Cover WebSocket connections, event handling, optimistic updates, and error recovery mechanisms. Include examples of status monitoring interfaces, progress indicators, and notification systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Start Procedure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Trip Start Procedure. Document the transition from accepted to active trip status, including passenger verification, pickup confirmation, and navigation integration. Explain the start trip API endpoint, GPS coordinate validation, fare calculation initialization, and real-time tracking activation. Include implementation details for passenger check-in procedures, route optimization, and trip progress monitoring. Address safety protocols, emergency contact features, and connectivity fallback mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Domain-Specific Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for domain-specific UI components including trip cards, driver profiles, passenger lists, earnings displays, analytics charts, and navigation elements. Document specialized props for ride-sharing functionality like trip status indicators, location markers, rating systems, and real-time updates. Include integration examples with application data models and state management. Address performance optimization for large datasets and real-time data rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Broadcasting & Message Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the event broadcasting system that manages real-time message distribution across connected clients. Document event types, message formats, subscription patterns, and broadcast routing mechanisms. Include event filtering, payload validation, and delivery guarantees. Address message queuing, retry logic, and dead letter handling. Provide examples of custom event creation, client-side event listeners, and performance optimization for high-volume messaging scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Permission Checking & Route Protection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the permission checking system and route protection mechanisms. Document how Supabase middleware validates user sessions and checks permissions before granting access to protected routes. Explain the implementation of server-side authentication guards, client-side navigation protection, and API endpoint authorization. Include examples of protected route patterns, custom permission checkers, and error handling for unauthorized access. Address session management, token validation, and security best practices for protecting sensitive endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Status Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for trip status management endpoints covering the complete trip lifecycle. Document driver acceptance workflows, trip initiation processes, completion handling, and cancellation procedures. Include detailed specifications for each status transition (pending → accepted → in-progress → completed/cancelled), required permissions, and state validation rules. Provide concrete examples of status update requests, real-time notifications, and error handling for invalid state transitions. Document concurrent access controls and conflict resolution mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Passenger Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for passenger management endpoints covering passenger data operations and profile management. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for passenger CRUD operations, profile updates, account settings, and user data retrieval. Include detailed specifications for passenger registration validation, profile editing capabilities, preference management, and account status modifications. Provide concrete examples of passenger data queries, profile update operations, bulk passenger management tasks, and privacy-compliant data handling. Document role-based access controls and data protection measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Account Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for driver account status management features. Document the blocking and unblocking mechanisms, including temporary suspensions and permanent bans. Explain the status lifecycle states (active, blocked, suspended, pending) and their implications on driver operations. Detail the block/unblock API endpoints, authorization requirements, and bulk status update capabilities. Include the driver detail page showing current status, history of status changes, and reason logging. Address notification systems when driver status changes, impact on active trips, and data retention policies for blocked accounts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Passenger Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Passenger Application, the main booking interface for ride-sharing passengers. Document the user interface components, trip booking workflow, real-time tracking implementation, payment processing integration, and driver discovery system. Explain the authentication flow, route protection, state management patterns, and API integrations. Include detailed coverage of the home page functionality, trip request handling, nearby driver search algorithms, live location tracking, rating system, and payment gateway integration. Address responsive design considerations, performance optimization, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the application layer design in Ubar. Describe the separation of concerns between passenger, driver, and admin applications, each serving distinct user roles with specialized interfaces and workflows. Document the common architectural patterns including Next.js App Router usage, Server Components implementation, component composition strategies, and provider patterns for global state management. Explain how each application maintains its own routing structure while sharing infrastructure through packages. Include diagrams showing application boundaries, data flow between apps, and shared service consumption patterns. Address authentication context sharing, API route organization, and deployment considerations for multi-application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Discovery & Matching
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the driver discovery and matching system. Document the nearby driver search algorithm, location-based filtering, distance calculations, and availability checking mechanisms. Explain the geospatial queries, performance optimization for large datasets, and real-time driver status updates. Cover the API endpoint for driver discovery, response formatting, pagination strategies, and caching mechanisms. Include examples of map integration, driver listing components, and matching criteria configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Earnings & Analytics System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Earnings Tracking and Analytics system. Document earnings calculation algorithms, payment processing integration, daily/weekly/monthly earnings reports, performance metrics, and financial analytics. Explain the earnings data model, calculation formulas, tax considerations, and withdrawal processing. Include implementation details for earnings visualization, chart rendering, data aggregation, and export functionality. Address currency handling, payment gateway integration, and financial reporting compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Analytics & Reporting system, covering revenue tracking, performance metrics, and business intelligence dashboards. Document trip analytics including booking trends, completion rates, and cancellation patterns. Explain financial reporting features such as earnings distribution, commission calculations, and payout summaries. Detail user engagement metrics, geographic distribution analysis, and peak usage pattern identification. Include export capabilities, custom report generation, and data visualization tools. Address data aggregation strategies, performance optimization for large datasets, and reporting schedule automation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Functions & Helpers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Shared Utils package containing utility functions and helper methods used throughout the Ubar applications. Document date formatting utilities, currency calculations, validation helpers, string manipulation functions, mathematical calculations for distance and pricing, and common business logic helpers. Include function signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and practical usage examples. Address error handling patterns, testing approaches, and performance considerations for frequently used utilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for session management across all applications. Document token storage strategies, session persistence mechanisms, and cross-application session sharing. Explain how sessions are created during login, refreshed automatically, and securely terminated during logout. Cover session validation, expiration handling, and concurrent session management. Include examples of session state management in React components, server-side session verification, and secure cookie handling. Address session security, CSRF protection, and session hijacking prevention measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the real-time trip status management system. Document trip state transitions, status broadcasting mechanisms, and event-driven updates between drivers and passengers. Explain the trip lifecycle from booking to completion, including acceptance, start, progress, and completion events. Cover status synchronization, conflict resolution, and offline handling strategies. Include examples of implementing trip status listeners, handling status changes, and managing concurrent trip operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Refund Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the refund processing system handling trip cancellations, service failures, and customer disputes. Document refund eligibility criteria, partial vs full refund scenarios, and automated refund triggers. Explain refund calculation methods, fee deductions, and processing timelines. Cover refund status tracking, notification systems, and customer communication workflows. Include documentation for refund approval workflows, admin overrides, and dispute resolution processes. Address refund auditing, compliance requirements, and financial reconciliation procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for payment processing endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for payment initiation, processing, confirmation, and refund handling. Include detailed specifications for payment gateway integration, transaction recording, receipt generation, and financial reporting. Provide concrete examples of payment requests, transaction responses, webhook notifications, and error handling for failed payments. Document security requirements, PCI compliance considerations, and payment state management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Cancellation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the trip cancellation system. Document the cancellation workflow, business rules, refund processing, and notification systems. Explain the cancellation API endpoints, validation rules, timing constraints, and penalty calculations. Cover the integration with payment systems for refunds, driver notifications, and reassignment processes. Include examples of cancellation confirmation dialogs, refund status tracking, and policy enforcement mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Completion Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Trip Completion Process. Document how trips are finalized, including drop-off confirmation, fare calculation, payment processing, and rating submission. Explain the complete trip API endpoint, earnings update mechanism, receipt generation, and performance metrics recording. Include code examples for finalizing trips, calculating tips, updating driver statistics, and handling post-trip workflows. Address refund scenarios, dispute resolution, and quality assurance processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout & Navigation Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for layout and navigation components including sidebars, headers, breadcrumbs, routing wrappers, and responsive layouts. Document component composition patterns, route guards, and authentication-aware navigation. Include examples of role-based navigation differences between passenger, driver, and admin interfaces. Address mobile-responsive behavior, accessibility compliance, and performance optimization for complex navigation structures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Location Tracking Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the location tracking service that enables real-time driver position updates and passenger trip tracking. Document location data structure, update frequency optimization, geospatial queries, and proximity calculations. Include driver location broadcasting, passenger location consumption, map integration patterns, and battery optimization strategies. Address location accuracy, privacy considerations, and fallback mechanisms when GPS is unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Specific Workflows & Approval Processes
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for role-specific workflows and approval processes within the Ubar platform. Document the driver onboarding workflow including registration, document verification, admin approval/rejection processes, and account activation. Explain passenger registration flows, admin user creation, and any special approval requirements. Include examples of state transitions, notification systems for approval updates, and audit logging for administrative actions. Address temporary states during approval processes, rejection reasons, and reapplication workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip History API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for trip history retrieval endpoints across passenger and driver applications. Document query parameters for filtering trips by date range, status, location, and user role. Include response schemas for historical trip data, pagination support, and sorting options. Provide examples of common queries such as recent trips, earnings reports for drivers, and ride history for passengers. Document data retention policies, performance considerations for large datasets, and caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for profile management endpoints covering user authentication, registration, and profile operations across all user types. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for login/logout flows, user registration processes, profile data updates, and authentication state management. Include detailed specifications for role-specific registration workflows, password management, session handling, and profile synchronization. Provide concrete examples of authentication requests, profile update operations, multi-role registration flows, and secure session management. Document security considerations, input validation, and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Profile & Vehicle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for driver profile and vehicle information management. Document the driver profile editing capabilities, including personal information updates, contact details management, and document uploads. Explain vehicle information management including vehicle registration, insurance documents, inspection certificates, and vehicle type categorization. Detail the admin interface for viewing and editing driver profiles, verifying uploaded documents, and managing vehicle records. Include validation rules, file upload restrictions, and data synchronization between driver self-service and admin interfaces. Address privacy considerations for sensitive personal and vehicle data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Driver Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Driver Application, the interface for drivers to manage their ridesharing activities. Document the dashboard functionality, trip acceptance workflow, earnings tracking system, location broadcasting mechanism, and trip history management. Explain the driver approval process, availability status management, navigation integration, and performance analytics. Include detailed coverage of real-time trip updates, earnings calculation algorithms, location tracking implementation, and driver profile management. Address mobile-responsive design, battery optimization, and background location services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the data flow patterns in Ubar system. Describe how data moves through the application layers from client requests to database operations and back. Document the Prisma ORM integration pattern used across all applications, Supabase client configuration for server-side operations, and real-time data synchronization mechanisms. Explain the request-response lifecycle, caching strategies, and data consistency patterns. Include diagrams showing typical data flows for key operations like trip booking, user authentication, and real-time location updates. Address error handling, data validation, and transaction management across the distributed application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the payment processing system in the passenger application. Document the payment gateway integration, transaction processing workflow, payment method management, and receipt generation. Explain the payment initiation flow, success/failure handling, refund processing, and financial data security measures. Cover the payment API endpoints, webhook handling, error recovery strategies, and compliance considerations. Include examples of payment forms, transaction status displays, and rating/review integration with payments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Location Broadcasting & Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Location Broadcasting and Tracking system. Document real-time location sharing, GPS coordinate broadcasting, location accuracy handling, and battery optimization strategies. Explain the location update mechanism, WebSocket connections, geofencing capabilities, and privacy controls. Include implementation details for location caching, offline handling, background location services, and performance optimization. Address location accuracy validation, privacy compliance, and integration with mapping services.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the System Configuration interface, covering platform-wide settings and administrative controls. Document pricing configuration including base fares, per-mile rates, surge pricing rules, and promotional discount management. Explain service area configuration, vehicle type definitions, and driver requirements setup. Detail notification templates, email/SMS configuration, and third-party service integrations. Include feature flag management, maintenance mode controls, and environment-specific settings. Address configuration validation, backup/restore procedures, and change auditing for system modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Real-time Communication Services package that handles live updates and WebSocket connections across all Ubar applications. Document connection management, event broadcasting, location tracking services, trip status synchronization, and real-time notification systems. Include connection lifecycle management, error handling and reconnection strategies, message formats, and event subscription patterns. Address scalability considerations, connection pooling, and monitoring real-time service health.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for security best practices implemented throughout the authentication system. Document input validation, sanitization techniques, and SQL injection prevention. Explain password hashing, secure credential storage, and sensitive data handling. Cover CORS configuration, rate limiting, and brute force attack prevention. Include examples of secure API endpoint implementation, error handling that doesn't leak sensitive information, and security headers configuration. Address common vulnerabilities like XSS, CSRF, and their mitigation strategies within the Next.js and Supabase context.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Events & Notifications
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the real-time event system and notification framework. Explain event types, message formats, and event routing mechanisms. Cover notification delivery, priority handling, and user preference management. Document the event subscription patterns, payload structures, and error handling for failed deliveries. Include examples of creating custom events, subscribing to notifications, and implementing event handlers across different applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Reporting & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for financial reporting and analytics capabilities across all user roles. Document revenue tracking, transaction summaries, and financial dashboards for passengers, drivers, and administrators. Explain report generation, data export formats, and scheduled reporting features. Cover financial metrics calculation, trend analysis, and performance indicators. Include documentation for audit trails, compliance reporting, and regulatory requirements. Address data visualization components, custom report builders, and integration with external accounting systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for user management endpoints covering driver administration, passenger management, and profile operations. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for user CRUD operations, approval workflows, profile updates, and account management. Include detailed specifications for driver approval/rejection processes, passenger data management, profile editing capabilities, and user status modifications. Provide concrete examples of user creation requests, approval workflows, profile update operations, and bulk user management tasks. Document role-based access control and permission validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip History Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for trip history tracking and retrieval. Document the historical data storage, query optimization, pagination strategies, and filtering capabilities. Explain the API endpoints for retrieving trip history, data aggregation methods, and performance considerations for large datasets. Cover the user interface components for displaying trip history, search functionality, and export features. Include examples of history filtering, sorting options, and detailed trip view components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for Trip State Management. Document the trip state machine, including all possible states (pending, accepted, started, completed, cancelled) and valid state transitions. Explain database schema design, state persistence, concurrent access handling, and data consistency guarantees. Include implementation details for state validation, rollback mechanisms, and audit logging. Address state synchronization across clients, conflict resolution strategies, and recovery from partial failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling & Theming System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the comprehensive styling and theming system used across all Ubar applications. Explain CSS architecture, design tokens, color schemes, typography scales, and spacing systems. Include guidelines for creating custom themes, overriding default styles, and maintaining visual consistency. Address responsive design breakpoints, dark mode support, and cross-browser compatibility. Provide examples of extending the theme system for new applications or custom branding requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Status Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the trip status synchronization system that keeps all parties informed about trip lifecycle events in real-time. Document status transitions, event propagation between passengers and drivers, conflict resolution strategies, and offline state handling. Include trip acceptance workflows, start/complete notifications, cancellation handling, and status persistence. Address race conditions, optimistic updates, and rollback mechanisms for failed operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip Rating System API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the trip rating and review system. Document rating submission endpoints, validation rules for ratings and reviews, and response formats. Include examples of rating submissions from passengers, rating display APIs, and rating aggregation logic. Document constraints such as one-way rating systems, rating ranges, review text limits, and moderation workflows. Address privacy considerations, rating visibility rules, and impact on driver/passenger profiles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Admin Application, the administrative dashboard for platform oversight and management. Document the administrative dashboard, user management systems, analytics and reporting capabilities, and system configuration interfaces. Explain driver approval workflows, passenger account management, real-time monitoring tools, and revenue reporting features. Include detailed coverage of role-based access control, content moderation tools, system settings management, and performance monitoring dashboards. Address security considerations, audit logging, and administrative best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Location Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the real-time location tracking system. Document the live driver location updates, trip progress monitoring, ETA calculations, and map integration patterns. Explain the WebSocket connections, event handling for location changes, performance optimization for frequent updates, and fallback mechanisms for connection failures. Cover the real-time data synchronization, state management for live updates, and user interface responsiveness during tracking. Include examples of map components, location update handlers, and tracking status indicators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile & Settings Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Driver Profile and Settings Management system. Document profile information management, vehicle details, document upload and verification, preference settings, and account configuration. Explain the profile data model, image upload handling, document verification workflow, and settings persistence. Include implementation details for file uploads, validation rules, security considerations, and user experience flows. Address document expiration handling, profile completeness checks, and regulatory compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema & Migrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Database Schema and Prisma configuration that manages the centralized database structure across all Ubar applications. Document the Prisma schema definition, entity relationships, database constraints, and migration strategies. Explain user models, trip management entities, payment records, and review systems. Include database connection configuration, query optimization patterns, and data access best practices. Address migration workflows, rollback procedures, and database performance tuning guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Location Tracking API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for location tracking and nearby driver discovery endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for location broadcasting, nearby driver queries, and real-time position updates. Include detailed specifications for GPS coordinate handling, distance calculations, driver availability filtering, and proximity search algorithms. Provide concrete examples of location update requests, nearby driver search parameters, response formats with driver details, and performance optimization techniques. Document rate limiting for location updates and privacy considerations for location data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Notification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the real-time notification system that delivers instant alerts and updates to users across all applications. Document notification types, priority levels, user preferences, and delivery channels. Include push notification integration, in-app notification display, notification history, and user engagement tracking. Address notification batching, rate limiting, and graceful degradation when real-time services are unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Shared Infrastructure layer that provides common functionality across all Ubar applications. Document the shared TypeScript types and interfaces, reusable UI components library, utility functions and helpers, real-time communication services, and centralized database schema management. Explain the package architecture, dependency management, build processes, and distribution strategies. Include detailed coverage of type definitions, component APIs, utility function catalogs, real-time event handling, and Prisma schema organization. Address versioning strategies, backward compatibility, and contribution guidelines for shared packages.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the user interface architecture and navigation patterns of the passenger application. Explain the layout structure, component hierarchy, styling approach, and responsive design implementation. Cover the toolbar components, page transitions, loading states, and error boundary implementations. Document the CSS architecture, theme configuration, accessibility features, and cross-browser compatibility considerations. Include examples of reusable components, navigation guards, and UI state management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trip History & Records
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Trip History and Records system. Document trip history retrieval, filtering and search capabilities, trip details display, rating management, and historical analytics. Explain the data pagination strategy, search algorithms, performance optimization for large datasets, and export functionality. Include implementation details for trip status filtering, date range queries, rating displays, and revenue summaries. Address data retention policies, archival strategies, and performance considerations for historical data access.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Analytics & Settings API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for administrative analytics and system settings endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for analytics data retrieval, system configuration, and platform monitoring. Include detailed specifications for revenue reporting, user statistics, performance metrics, and system health checks. Provide concrete examples of analytics queries, configuration updates, dashboard data requests, and system status monitoring. Document data aggregation methods, caching strategies, and performance considerations for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Ubar database schema using Prisma ORM. Detail entity relationships between users, drivers, passengers, trips, payments, and reviews. Document field definitions, data types, constraints, indexes, and foreign key relationships. Explain the database design decisions, normalization strategies, and query optimization approaches. Include database schema diagrams, sample queries, migration management, and data validation rules. Address performance considerations, caching strategies, and data integrity measures. Document backup procedures, disaster recovery, and data retention policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication and authorization system built on Supabase. Document the role-based access control implementation, session management, and security patterns across all three applications. Explain the authentication flows for passengers, drivers, and administrators, including registration, login, password reset, and account verification processes. Include detailed coverage of middleware implementations, route protection, permission checks, and security best practices. Address token management, session persistence, and cross-application authentication sharing. Document security considerations, vulnerability mitigation, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all RESTful endpoints across the Ubar platform. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error codes for all API routes. Include detailed specifications for authentication endpoints, trip management APIs, payment processing endpoints, user management APIs, and real-time event handlers. Provide concrete examples of request payloads, response formats, and error responses. Document rate limiting, versioning strategies, and API best practices. Address WebSocket connections for real-time features, message formats, and connection management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the real-time communication system powering live location tracking, trip status updates, and instant notifications. Document the WebSocket implementation, real-time event handling, connection management, and data synchronization patterns. Explain the location broadcasting system, driver-passenger matching algorithms, and real-time trip status propagation. Include detailed coverage of connection pooling, error handling, reconnection strategies, and performance optimization. Address scalability considerations, load balancing, and monitoring approaches for real-time features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the payment processing system handling transactions, earnings distribution, and financial reporting. Document payment gateway integration, transaction lifecycle management, refund processing, and earnings calculation algorithms. Explain payment method handling, currency support, tax calculations, and fee structures. Include detailed coverage of payment security, PCI compliance, transaction auditing, and financial reconciliation processes. Address payment failure handling, retry mechanisms, and dispute resolution workflows. Document reporting capabilities, financial analytics, and export functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the testing strategy and implementation across the Ubar monorepo. Document unit testing approaches for shared packages, integration testing for API endpoints, and end-to-end testing for user workflows. Explain testing frameworks, test organization, mocking strategies, and continuous integration setup. Include detailed coverage of test data management, environment configuration, and performance testing approaches. Address testing best practices, code coverage requirements, and debugging techniques for failed tests. Document testing utilities, helper functions, and reusable test components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for deployment strategies and DevOps practices for the Ubar platform. Document build pipelines, environment configuration, containerization strategies, and CI/CD workflows. Explain deployment targets, scaling considerations, monitoring setup, and logging strategies. Include detailed coverage of environment variable management, secrets handling, and configuration management across multiple applications. Address performance optimization, caching strategies, CDN configuration, and database deployment procedures. Document rollback procedures, disaster recovery, and maintenance windows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues, solutions, and frequently asked questions for the Ubar platform. Document debugging techniques for each application, common error messages and their resolutions, performance troubleshooting guides, and database connectivity issues. Include detailed coverage of real-time connection problems, authentication failures, payment processing errors, and deployment issues. Address monitoring and logging strategies, diagnostic tools, and log analysis techniques. Provide step-by-step troubleshooting procedures for critical system components and integration points.

wiki_overview.content:
</think>

<blog>

# Ubar - Multi-Role Ride Sharing Platform

## Project Introduction

Ubar is a comprehensive ride-sharing platform built as a monorepo architecture, providing distinct interfaces for three primary user roles: passengers, drivers, and administrators. The project follows modern web development practices using Next.js with Server Components and implements a role-based access control system to serve different user experiences within a unified codebase.

### Purpose Statement
The platform aims to connect passengers with available drivers in real-time while providing administrative oversight and driver management capabilities through dedicated interfaces.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Multi-role Architecture**: Separate applications for passengers, drivers, and admins
- **Real-time Communication**: Live location tracking and trip updates
- **Secure Authentication**: Role-based access control with Supabase integration
- **Database Management**: Centralized data layer using Prisma ORM
- **Shared Components**: Reusable UI components and utilities across applications

### Target Audience
- **Passengers**: Users seeking transportation services
- **Drivers**: Service providers offering rides
- **Administrators**: Platform operators managing the ecosystem

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a monorepo structure with clear separation of concerns and shared resources:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Monorepo Root] --> B[apps/]
    A --> C[packages/]
    
    B --> D[admin/]
    B --> E[driver/]
    B --> F[passenger/]
    
    C --> G[prisma/]
    C --> H[real-time/]
    C --> I[shared-types/]
    C --> J[shared-ui/]
    C --> K[shared-utils/]
    
    D --> L[Admin Dashboard]
    E --> M[Driver Interface]
    F --> N[Passenger App]
    
    L --> O[Supabase Auth]
    M --> O
    N --> O
    
    L --> P[Prisma Database]
    M --> P
    N --> P
```

### Component Breakdown

**Application Layer:**
- **Admin Application**: Comprehensive dashboard for platform management
- **Driver Application**: Driver-specific interface for trip management and earnings
- **Passenger Application**: User-facing app for booking and tracking rides

**Shared Infrastructure:**
- **Prisma Schema**: Centralized database models and relationships
- **Real-time Services**: WebSocket or similar technology for live updates
- **Shared Types**: TypeScript definitions ensuring type safety across apps
- **UI Components**: Reusable React components for consistent design
- **Utilities**: Common functions and helpers used across applications

### Design Patterns

1. **Monorepo Pattern**: Single repository managing multiple related projects
2. **Server Components**: Next.js Server Components for improved performance
3. **API Routes**: File-based routing with server-side API endpoints
4. **Role-Based Access Control**: Different authentication flows per user type
5. **Shared Package Strategy**: Common functionality extracted into reusable packages

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Passenger as Passenger App
    participant Driver as Driver App
    participant Admin as Admin App
    participant Auth as Supabase Auth
    participant DB as Prisma Database
    participant RealTime as Real-time Service
    
    Passenger->>Auth: Login/Register
    Driver->>Auth: Login/Register
    Admin->>Auth: Admin Login
    
    Passenger->>DB: Request Trip
    DB->>RealTime: Update Location
    RealTime->>Driver: Notify Available Trips
    Driver->>DB: Accept Trip
    DB->>Passenger: Confirm Booking
    
    Admin->>DB: Manage Users
    Admin->>RealTime: Monitor Activity
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

Each application follows Next.js conventions with dedicated entry points:

- **Admin App**: `apps/admin/src/app/page.tsx` - Main admin dashboard entry
- **Driver App**: `apps/driver/src/app/page.tsx` - Driver landing page
- **Passenger App**: `apps/passenger/src/app/page.tsx` - Passenger home screen

### Core Modules

**Authentication System:**
- `apps/*/src/lib/supabase.ts` - Client-side Supabase configuration
- `apps/*/src/lib/supabase-server.ts` - Server-side Supabase client
- `apps/*/src/app/api/auth/` - Authentication API routes

**Database Layer:**
- `packages/prisma/` - Centralized Prisma schema and migrations
- `apps/*/src/lib/prisma.ts` - Database connection configuration

**Real-time Features:**
- `packages/real-time/` - Real-time communication implementation
- Location tracking and trip status updates

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration:**
- `apps/*/next.config.ts` - Next.js specific configurations
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration at root level
- `package.json` - Dependency management and scripts

**Environment Management:**
- Supabase credentials and database URLs
- Real-time service endpoints
- Feature flags and environment-specific settings

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework:**
- Next.js 14+ with App Router
- React 18+ with Server Components
- TypeScript for type safety

**Backend Services:**
- Supabase for authentication and database
- Prisma ORM for database operations
- Real-time communication libraries

**Development Tools:**
- ESLint for code quality
- PostCSS for styling processing
- Node.js package manager

### Integration Points

**Supabase Integration:**
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Applications] --> B[Supabase Client]
    B --> C[Authentication]
    B --> D[Database]
    B --> E[Real-time]
    B --> F[Storage]
```

**Database Schema Relationships:**
```mermaid
erDiagram
    USERS ||--o{ DRIVERS : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ PASSENGERS : "has"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ TRIPS : "drives"
    PASSENGERS ||--o{ TRIPS : "books"
    TRIPS ||--o{ PAYMENTS : "generates"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ REVIEWS : "receives"
    PASSENGERS ||--o{ REVIEWS : "gives"
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Passenger Features:**
- User registration and authentication
- Nearby driver discovery
- Trip booking and cancellation
- Real-time trip tracking
- Payment processing
- Rating and review system
- Trip history and receipts

**Driver Features:**
- Driver onboarding and approval workflow
- Trip acceptance and navigation
- Earnings tracking and withdrawal
- Availability management
- Trip history and performance metrics
- Profile and vehicle information management

**Admin Features:**
- User management (drivers, passengers)
- Trip monitoring and analytics
- Revenue reporting and insights
- System configuration
- Content moderation
- Performance metrics dashboard

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Location Tracking:**
- `apps/passenger/src/app/home/page.tsx` - Live map interface
- `apps/driver/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` - Driver location broadcasting
- `packages/real-time/` - WebSocket or similar implementation

**Trip Management Workflow:**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Available
    Available --> Requested: Passenger Books
    Requested --> Accepted: Driver Accepts
    Accepted --> InProgress: Driver Starts
    InProgress --> Completed: Driver Finishes
    Completed --> Rated: Passenger Rates
    Rated --> [*]: Payment Processed
    
    Requested --> Cancelled: Passenger Cancels
    Accepted --> Cancelled: Driver Cancels
    Cancelled --> [*]: Refund Processed
```

**Authentication Flow:**
- `apps/*/src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts` - Login endpoint
- `apps/*/src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts` - Registration endpoint
- Role-based middleware and route protection

**Payment Processing:**
- `apps/passenger/src/app/api/payments/route.ts` - Payment handling
- Integration with payment gateways
- Transaction logging and receipt generation

### Feature Architecture

**Modular Component Structure:**
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Shared UI Components] --> B[Passenger Components]
    A --> C[Driver Components]
    A --> D[Admin Components]
    
    E[Shared Utils] --> B
    E --> C
    E --> D
    
    F[Real-time Services] --> B
    F --> C
    F --> D
    
    G[Database Models] --> B
    G --> C
    G --> D
```

**State Management:**
- React Context for global state
- Server Components for data fetching
- Client Components for interactive features
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates

---

**Sources:**
- [project structure](c:\Users\hp\Desktop\ubar)
- [monorepo configuration](c:\Users\hp\Desktop\ubar/package.json)
- [TypeScript configuration](c:\Users\hp\Desktop\ubar/tsconfig.json)

</blog>
